title: The Discourse to the Kalama : Discernment, the Ethical Life and Fake News.To play the media you will need to either update your browser to a recent version or update yourFlash plugin
justification: This talk provides the most comprehensive and structured treatment of the Kalama Sutta, combining a complete reading of the discourse with detailed commentary on each category of unreliable knowledge (tradition, reason, authority). It uniquely connects the ancient teaching to contemporary issues like fake news, the Brexit referendum, and cognitive biases, making it highly relevant and engaging. The talk includes rich anecdotes (the elephant story, Chinese whispers exercise) and practical applications while maintaining strong pedagogical flow from the sutta's ethical foundations through the four brahmaviharas to the four consolations.
summary: Bhante Bodhidhamma delivers a thorough exposition of the Kalama Sutta, explaining the Buddha's ten categories of unreliable knowledge sources and connecting them to modern issues of discernment and fake news. The talk progresses through the complete discourse, covering ethical foundations, the practice of the brahmaviharas, and the four consolations that arise from right living.

relevance.note: Directly examines Kalama Sutta and its teachings
